title: Fuel and emission control structure description - Fuel cut control

blocks.text: Fuel and emission control structure description - Fuel cut control
Fuel injection is stopped in areas where the engine speed exceeds the set engine speed during deceleration near fully closed throttle valves after the engine is warmed up to improve fuel economy.

To protect the engine, stop fuel injection at the engine speed limit (red zone) when driving. However, when the vehicle is stopped, fuel injection is stopped at a lower engine speed than when the vehicle is running.
Fuel injection is stopped at high speeds of 180 km/h or more.
